refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall minimize energy consumption per ton moved to meet or exceed the customer-defined energy efficiency target.
refined_fr.rationale: Refining the high-level FR into a measurable and actionable sub-FR focused on minimizing energy per ton moved aligns with the acceptance criteria and enables targeted design and verification.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure total energy used and tons moved over the evaluation period and verify energy_per_ton ≤ E_target.
sub_frs: Accurately measure energy used and tons moved
Provide feedback to operator and system for energy efficiency

potential_sources: Interaction between control algorithms and powertrain
Data dependency between monitoring and control
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP logically isolates control adaptation from hardware design, minimizing coupling. Interfaces with monitoring are well-defined.

assumptions_risks: Accurate and timely energy and load data are available
Control algorithms can be updated without impacting safety
Adaptive control effectiveness depends on operator acceptance
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider integrating energy monitoring DP explicitly if data accuracy issues arise.

im_boundary.name: Vehicle
im_boundary.scope: Physical and embedded control systems responsible for operational control and energy management

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Exchange real-time energy usage and operational data with NGHE Solution and Teleoperation modules

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receive operational commands and efficiency targets from NGHE Solution

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Send safety-related alerts to Safety Services module

module_containment_scores.notes: High diffusion due to cross-cutting coordination role; low containment of embedded control logic
Strong containment of control algorithms and energy management; minimal diffusion
Limited containment; mainly remote control interface
Primarily safety alert reception; no control logic containment

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Embedding adaptive control algorithms within the Vehicle module ensures tight coupling with physical systems and minimizes diffusion across modules.
containment_risks: Potential complexity in Vehicle module may impact maintainability
Interface dependencies with NGHE Solution and Safety Services require robust interface management
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Monitor interface stability and consider modularizing control algorithms if diffusion increases.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The refined FR is well-defined and the selected DP logically satisfies the energy efficiency requirement with manageable coupling.

diffusion_risks: Cross-module data exchange via Telemetry interface may increase diffusion if not tightly controlled
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Vehicle module shows strong containment of control logic, minimizing diffusion risk.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed by measuring energy consumption and tons moved, with data collected via Telemetry interface.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of adaptive control DP to Vehicle IM with well-defined interfaces.

recommended_revisions: Review interface protocols for Telemetry and SafetyAlert to ensure robustness.
Plan for modularization if Vehicle module complexity grows.

decision.justification: Current level of decomposition and assignments satisfy the requirement with clear FR refinement, DP selection, and IM assignment. Risks are identified and manageable.
next_level_sub_frs: Accurately measure energy used and tons moved
Provide feedback to operator and system for energy efficiency

revise.why: No immediate revision needed; proceed to next decomposition level.

assumptions: Energy and load data are accurate and timely
Control algorithms can be safely updated
evidence_needed: Energy consumption and tonnage measurement data
